title: Dataset of neurons and intracranial EEG from human amygdala during aversive dynamic visual stimulation

funding_references.funder_name: Stiftung für wissenschaftliche Forschung an der Universität Zürich

description: This dataset comprises intracranial EEG recordings and single-unit neuronal activity from the human amygdala of nine epilepsy patients during exposure to aversive dynamic visual stimuli. Subjects viewed fearful faces (aversive condition) interleaved with neutral landscapes while undergoing preoperative invasive monitoring in the mesial temporal lobe. The dataset enables investigation of amygdalar responses to emotional stimuli across multiple spatial scales, from macroscopic field potentials to single-neuron firing patterns.
methods_description: Intracranial EEG and neuronal spike recordings were collected from nine epilepsy patients undergoing preoperative invasive monitoring of the mesial temporal lobe. Subjects were presented with dynamic visual sequences of fearful faces (aversive condition) interleaved with neutral landscape sequences (neutral condition). Data are provided in BIDS standard format, with extended neuronal data available in NIX format.
